cases.description: Logic Assign Priority Mux: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Logic Assign Priority Mux: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Logic Assign Priority Mux: mishandles reset or initialization, passing steady-state samples but failing startup or recovery checks.
Logic Assign Priority Mux: updates on the wrong edge or event, so simple static samples pass but timed checks fail.
Logic Assign Priority Mux: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
